commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow docs repair can correct the sub-agent tool-boundary wording without changing runtime behavior.; Needs attention: Docs-only PR with no supply-chain change, but the sub-agent wording understates tool exposure for local workers.; Review finding: [P2] Don't call native sub-agents text-only by default — docs/gateway/local-text-workers.md:106-108 (sha=8da52f5b800ddcb37f91e31956be1ff30aa68148)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a new Gateway docs page for the cloud-orchestrator plus local text-workers pattern and wires it into docs navigation, local-model related links, and the Chinese glossary.

PR surface: Docs +208. Total +208 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a docs PR, not a runtime bug. The high-confidence check is comparing the proposed wording against current sub-agent docs and runtime tool-policy source.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow docs repair can correct the sub-agent tool-boundary wording without changing runtime behavior.

target.title: docs(gateway): add cloud-orchestrator plus local-text-workers pattern
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a docs page for the cloud-orchestrator plus local-text-workers pattern: a tool-using cloud model orchestrates while local models do bounded text-only work (drafting, summarizing, refactoring).
- Composes existing guidance (local models, sub-agents, CLI backends, LLM task, lean mode, sandbox/timeout knobs) into one workflow. No new runtime behavior or config.
- Frames local workers as text-only helpers by default and tells readers not to expect autonomous local tool use unless configured and verified.

## Linked context

Closes #51333

The issue asks for a docs page documenting this pattern. ClawSweeper's review confirmed current main and the latest release document adjacent local-model, sub-agent, CLI-backend, timeout, and sandbox concepts, but provide no composed cookbook. This PR adds that composed page using only existing surfaces.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: missing docs page for the cloud-orchestrator + local-text-worker pattern; the new page must register in the docs navigation and pass the docs toolchain (MDX, links, i18n glossary).
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux (Node 24), running the repo docs toolchain that builds the published docs site.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm docs:list | grep local-text-workers
pnpm docs:check-mdx
pnpm docs:check-links
pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix: terminal/console output captured from the real docs toolchain run after applying this patch:

```text
$ pnpm docs:list | grep local-text-workers
gateway/local-text-workers.md - Pattern: cloud model orchestrates and uses tools; local models do bounded text-only work

$ pnpm docs:check-mdx
Docs MDX check passed (678 files, 5503ms).

$ pnpm docs:check-links
checked_internal_links=4469
broken_links=0

$ pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary
Checked changed English docs relative to 8ae1adfdccce746c07c829604338da60256bafcd.

$ git diff --check
(no output; exit 0)
```

- Observed result after fix: the new page resolves in `docs:list` under its real route, the MDX parser accepts all 678 docs, every internal link including the new cross-links resolves (`broken_links=0`), the i18n glossary guard passes after registering the new "Local text workers" label, and `git diff --check` is clean.
- What was not tested: no live Mintlify (`mint dev`) browser render was captured in this checkout; the docs MDX/link/list pipeline above is the same toolchain that gates the published site. No runtime, config, or code path is touched.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: docs-only change. Every config field shown in the page is copied verbatim from the cited existing docs pages, not invented.

## Tests and validation

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list`, `pnpm docs:check-mdx`, `pnpm docs:check-links`, `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No` (docs only)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: docs accuracy — a reader might assume local models can do autonomous tool use.

How is that risk mitigated? The page explicitly frames local workers as text-only by default and links to the existing lean-mode and `supportsTools: false` guidance for the tool-call-incompatibility case; all config snippets are taken verbatim from existing docs.

Current review state: maintainer review and CI.

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Proof above was run manually.
target.author: zengwen-dt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
